description: Parameterizes bounding functions (like Lyapunov functions for Markov chains or rate functions for large deviations) using deep neural networks, optimizing the bound via gradient descent.
analogical_connections: Using a neural net as a flexible, multi-dimensional clay mold to tightly fit the complex, jagged shape of the true analytical bounding function.
implementation_hypothesis: Train an MLP to satisfy the supermartingale condition for the LCS growth process. The expected value of the network provides an upper bound.
experiment_seed: Build a loss function that penalizes violations of the Lyapunov drift condition and train on sampled sequence extensions.
experimental_result: Neural Lyapunov approach interesting but requires verified neural network outputs.
